agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Sensitivity is the proportion of true positive cases identified among people who actually have the disease. It represents the likelihood that a test will detect a genuine case when one exists.  

\boxed{C}

The summary focuses only on clinically significant definitions related to detection accuracy within affected populations—specifically emphasizing how sensitivity quantifies successful identification of actual illness versus false negatives. 

\boxed{C}. Answer choice: C
